title: • Not good vs. evil. It’s momentum—how things get better or worse.
content:
• Not good vs. evil. It’s momentum—how things get better or worse.

Example of Scene Pulses

Scene Start Action Scene End
Hopeful Protagonist receives bad news Desperate
Angry Argument turns into reconciliation Relieved
Confused Discovers a clue Determined

Activity: Scene Pulse Mapping
1. Write a simple scene.
2. Mark what emotional state it starts in.
3. Mark how it changes by the end.
4. Rewrite it with a clearer shift in pulse.

3. Macro & Micro Dramatic Structure (Story as a Graph)

A narrative is like a waveform—it goes up and down with positive and negative shifts over time.
• Big Picture (Macro Pulse): The whole story has highs and lows.
• Small Picture (Micro Pulse): Each scene also has highs and lows.

Think of it like this:
• One full story = multiple waves of rising and falling action.
• Each scene = a mini-wave that contributes to the bigger picture.
context: From document: Essential Storytelling Principles for Derek — chunk 2 (chunk 2/5)
